definitions.evidence_boundary_record: A machine-readable record preserving what was claimed, what was not claimed, what evidence was referenced, what reliance was declared, and what unresolved gaps remained at a governance handoff.
definitions.non_claim: A declared boundary stating what the layer, record, checker, or downstream consumer does not assert.
definitions.claim_expansion: A downstream interpretation that turns a bounded structural record into a broader approval, truth, safety, compliance, authority, readiness, or sufficiency claim not made by the source artifact.
definitions.local_authority: The institutionally accountable authority outside Fork that may make legal, compliance, safety, risk, policy, or deployment decisions.

controlling_test: Interoperability may increase reviewability only when it preserves claim boundaries, non-claims, unresolved gaps, and local authority rather than silently transferring or expanding them.
interoperability_doctrine: Evidence may travel. Non-claims and unresolved gaps should travel with it. Authority remains local.

layer.vendor_layer_descriptor: Evidence-boundary infrastructure for AI-assisted governance workflows.

surfaces.scope: records supported claims, explicit non-claims, upstream reliance, verification scope, and non-transitivity at artifact creation or declaration time
records how a downstream consumer relies on, preserves, narrows, expands, or leaves unresolved a prior claim boundary
checks whether assembled claim-boundary and consumption records remain structurally resolvable without silently inheriting claims
declares how CCEC records may be referenced by GRC, audit, legal, risk, policy, model governance, vendor-risk, evidence, or dashboard systems without becoming approval or control signals

does: preserves machine-readable claim and non-claim boundaries
records declared upstream reliance and downstream consumption behavior
identifies unresolved pointers, unresolved gaps, and boundary-expansion conditions
supports structural checking of declared artifacts, examples, schemas, and generated outputs
supports independent review of whether a sealed record or assembled bundle still structurally verifies
supports non-transitivity by default: downstream consumers do not inherit unmade claims
does_not: does not operate as runtime control
does not operate as a policy engine
does not inspect opaque host-platform scoring behavior
does not police human prose, dashboard narrative framing, or institutional culture

claims_boundary.authoritative_layer_claim: Fork preserves and checks machine-readable evidence-boundary records that identify what was claimed, what was not claimed, what evidence was referenced, what reliance was declared, and whether downstream composition preserves or expands those boundaries.
claims_boundary.consumer_boundary_constraint: A Fork structural result may be used as a boundary-inspection record only. It must not be converted into approval, truth, safety, compliance, legal sufficiency, risk acceptance, policy satisfaction, deployment readiness, control effectiveness, or authority transfer.

fork_responsibility: define schemas for claim-boundary and consumption records
provide structural checkers for declared artifacts
preserve explicit non-claims and unresolved gaps
detect declared boundary expansion and prohibited mapping patterns
external_responsibility: configure host-platform scoring, workflow, and dashboard behavior
audit human prose and executive narrative framing
responsibility_boundaries.authority_rule: Fork may preserve and inspect declared authority references, but authority remains with the local institution or accountable decision owner.

composition_assumptions: adjacent layers may preserve references to Fork artifacts without inheriting Fork non-claims as approvals
a downstream expansion requires a separate authority-bearing claim boundary or local decision record
composition is unilateral unless the adjacent layer publishes its own compatible manifest
absence of a reciprocal manifest must not be interpreted as mutual interoperability
composition.non_reciprocity_notice: This Fork declaration does not claim that any adjacent layer has declared reciprocal interoperability with Fork.

glm_compatibility.change_policy: breaking semantic changes are published as new manifest versions and tagged in the public repository

expansion_risks.mitigation: Claim Consumption Event requires preserved non-claims and boundary effect declaration
unresolved pointers and unresolved gaps remain explicit structural states
documented as conformance-review-only host obligation, not Fork runtime guarantee
exit zero is documented as process completion, not approval or readiness

vendor_framework_constructs.label: Claim Boundary Contract
vendor_framework_constructs.purpose: Declares the source-side boundary around supported claims, explicit non-claims, reliance, verification scope, and non-transitivity.

vendor_framework_constructs.label: Claim Consumption Event
vendor_framework_constructs.purpose: Records receiving-side reliance, preserved non-claims, unresolved gaps, boundary effects, and local decision ownership.

vendor_framework_constructs.label: Relational Graph Verifier
vendor_framework_constructs.purpose: Checks whether assembled claim and consumption records remain structurally resolvable without silent claim inheritance.

vendor_framework_constructs.label: CCEC Governance Interoperability Profile
vendor_framework_constructs.purpose: Declares how CCEC records may be referenced by surrounding governance systems without becoming approval, routing, scoring, SLA, readiness, or dashboard assurance signals.

manifest_digest.canonicalization_method: repo-local JSON canonicalization using sorted keys and compact separators, excluding manifest_digest to avoid self-reference
